ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'tea beverage' class (FOODON_03301099); no specific class exists for green tea with tapioca pearls / bubble tea. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJR covers green tea as a beverage category. No specific bubble tea or pearl tea code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent match to green tea base used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for green tea with tapioca pearls. SNOMED CT coverage for specialty beverage preparations is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of green tea with tapioca pearls is approximately 350 g (one takeaway cup), providing about 102 kcal, 25.7 g carbohydrate, and 15.6 g sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Green Tea with Pearls — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D08010043 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Per-100g values are taken as-is from the HPB record. Sodium was reported as -1 in the source, indicating the value was not available in the HPB database for this preparation.

gi_source.title: Tapioca and green tea beverage — analogous estimate based on tapioca pearls (GI ~60–70) and unsweetened green tea (GI negligible); composite GI estimated at 60 (medium) weighted by carbohydrate contribution of tapioca pearls
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for green tea with tapioca pearls in the University of Sydney GI Database. Tapioca pearls (cooked) have a published GI of approximately 60–70 (medium) per the University of Sydney GI Database. Unsweetened green tea contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially affect GI. The composite GI is estimated at 60 (medium), dominated by the tapioca pearl starch component. Glycemic load per 350 g serving = (60 × 25.7 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 15.4, rounded to 15.7 accounting for sugar contribution. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available.

hypertension.key_factor: Added sugar content (4.45 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage; sodium data unavailable but tea base is inherently low-sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid sugar-sweetened beverages including this preparation; request an unsweetened green tea base without sugar syrup, as the AHA 2018 guidelines recommend minimising added-sugar beverage intake to reduce cardiovascular and blood-pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 4.45 g sugar/100 g and high-GI tapioca pearls (GI ~60–70) providing 7.35 g carbohydrate/100 g with negligible dietary fibre (0.03 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This beverage is not recommended for individuals with type 2 diabetes because it combines added sugar syrup with high-GI tapioca pearls and near-zero dietary fibre, triggering the ADA sugar-sweetened beverage hard gate; if consuming a tea-based drink, choose plain unsweetened green tea without pearls or syrup.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar (4.45 g/100 g) in a sweetened beverage; fat is 0 g and cholesterol is 0 g, which are favourable, but regular SSB consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides and CVD risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Fat and cholesterol content are negligible and not a CVD concern; however, the added sugar content classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage, and regular consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk per AHA dietary guidelines — limit intake and prefer unsweetened green tea.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Green tea catechins provide polyphenol-based anti-inflammatory benefit; fat is 0 g and trans fat is absent
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The green tea base provides anti-inflammatory catechins beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis; however, added sugar syrup and tapioca pearls contribute to inflammatory load and should be minimised — request reduced or no sugar syrup and consider reducing pearl quantity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Green Tea with Tapioca Pearls (珍珠绿茶) is a popular cold or iced specialty beverage widely sold at bubble tea shops across Singapore and Southeast Asia. It consists of a brewed green tea base sweetened with sugar syrup and served with chewy cooked tapioca pearls (boba) at the bottom of the cup. The green tea base contributes a mild, slightly grassy, and lightly astringent flavour, while the tapioca pearls add a soft, chewy, and slightly sweet textural contrast. Per 100 g, the beverage provides 29 kcal, 7.35 g carbohydrate, 4.45 g sugar, 0.03 g dietary fibre, 0 g protein, and 0 g fat. Protein is naturally zero as this is a tea-based beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients. Fat is naturally zero by the nature of the preparation — green tea and tapioca starch contain no fat. The low energy density (29 kcal/100 g) reflects the high water content of the beverage. Clinically, this drink is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) due to the added sugar syrup, making it unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes or hypertension who should avoid SSBs. The tapioca pearls are high-GI refined starch with negligible fibre, contributing to rapid postprandial glucose elevation. The composite GI is estimated at approximately 60 (medium), dominated by the tapioca pearl starch. Sodium data is not available from the HPB source for this preparation. Green tea catechins in the base may offer mild anti-inflammatory benefits relevant to conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis. This beverage is vegan, dairy-free, and gluten-free. It is commonly consumed as a snack or refreshment throughout the day in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Input record is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id D08010043) with review_status reviewed and confidence 0.95. Per-100g macronutrient values are taken directly from the HPB canonical record. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for tapioca pearls, which is the dominant carbohydrate contributor.
notes: 1. ZERO MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): protein_g is naturally zero — this is a tea-based beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients (green tea and tapioca starch contain no protein). fat_g is naturally zero — green tea and tapioca starch contain no fat; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo record D08010043. saturated_fat_g and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero by the same source. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): sodium_mg is set to -1 because the HPB Singapore Foodo source record for D08010043 reports sodium as -1.0, indicating the value was not measured or not available for this preparation. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed micronutrient and fatty acid profiles for this specific green tea with tapioca pearls preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. GI NOTE: Glycemic index of 60 is an analogous estimate based on cooked tapioca pearls (GI ~60–70, University of Sydney GI Database) as the dominant carbohydrate contributor; no direct GI measurement for this specific beverage preparation was found. 4. ONTOLOGY NOTE: No exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for green tea with tapioca pearls / bubble tea; approximate matches to general tea beverage classes are used. 5. DIETARY TAGS from input: low_sodium tag from input is noted but sodium value is unavailable (-1); low_calorie tag is supported by 29 kcal/100 g.
